questions.question: What problem does debouncing solve for a search input?
options: It prevents the browser from blocking the main thread during heavy filtering
It stops the input from firing an API call on every keystroke when the user hasn't finished typing
It batches multiple simultaneous searches into a single network request
It cancels in-flight requests when a new character is typed

questions.question: In the inline debounce pattern, what triggers the actual search (e.g., updates `debouncedQuery`)?
options: The `@input` event on the text field after each keystroke
A `setTimeout` callback that fires after a period of inactivity in typing
A computed property that compares the raw and debounced values

questions.question: When would you choose throttle over debounce?
options: For search inputs where you want to fire once when the user finishes typing
For scroll or resize handlers where you want periodic updates during continuous activity
When the event fires at most once and debouncing would add unnecessary delay
When you need to cancel a pending API call before firing a new one
